era_context: The year 517 CE falls within the reign of Emperor Anastasius I in the Eastern Roman Empire (r. 491-518 CE), the Ostrogothic Kingdom under Theodoric in Italy, the Merovingian Frankish kingdoms in Western Europe, and the Liang Dynasty in southern China. This period is characterized by religious ferment (Monophysite-Chalcedonian disputes), military competition, and the consolidation of early Christian monasticism. The year immediately precedes the accession of Justin I, which would mark a significant shift in imperial policy.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Sasanid Empire (limited written sources for this specific year)

events.title: Vitalian's rebellion continues against Anastasius I
events.description: Vitalian's rebellion against Emperor Anastasius I continued in 517 CE, with the military commander and Chalcedonian Christian zealot maintaining control of much of the Balkans and Thrace. The rebellion, which had begun in 513 CE, posed a significant threat to imperial authority and reflected deep religious divisions within the empire. Vitalian's forces would continue to challenge imperial control until the rebellion was finally suppressed in 519-520 CE under Justin I.

sources.name: Procopius, History of the Wars (written c. 550 CE)
Evagrius Scholasticus, Ecclesiastical History (written c. 590 CE)

events.certainty_note: Vitalian's rebellion is well-documented as a continuous conflict from 513-520 CE, but specific events in 517 CE are not precisely documented. The assignment to this year is based on the broader chronology of the rebellion.
cross_references: Part of broader religious and military conflicts in early 6th-century Eastern Roman Empire; relates to Nika Riots (532 CE)

events.title: Theodoric the Ostrogoth rules Italy in relative stability
events.description: Theodoric the Ostrogoth (r. 493-526 CE) continued to rule Italy in 517 CE, maintaining relative stability and cultural synthesis between Ostrogothic and Roman traditions. Theodoric's reign was characterized by administrative competence and respect for Roman institutions, though tensions with the Eastern Roman Empire were gradually increasing. The year 517 CE falls within the relatively stable middle period of Theodoric's reign, before the conflicts that would lead to his death in 526 CE and the subsequent Ostrogothic-Byzantine war.
key_figures: Theodoric the Ostrogoth (King of the Ostrogoths, r. 493-526 CE)

sources.name: Cassiodorus, Variae (administrative letters, compiled early 6th century)
Procopius, History of the Wars (written c. 550 CE)

events.certainty_note: Theodoric's rule in 517 CE is attested in administrative records, but no specific events for this year are documented. The assignment reflects the broader context of his reign.
cross_references: Part of Theodoric's reign (493-526 CE); precedes Boethius's execution (520 CE) and Justinian's reconquest (535-554 CE)

events.title: Liang Dynasty Buddhist cultural flourishing continues
events.description: Emperor Wu of Liang (r. 502-549 CE) continued his patronage of Buddhism in 517 CE, supporting temple construction and the translation of Buddhist texts. The Liang court remained a major centre of Buddhist learning and cultural activity. This period saw the flourishing of Buddhist art, architecture, and scholarship in southern China.

sources.name: Liang Shu (History of the Liang Dynasty, compiled 7th century)
Buddhist biographical texts and temple records

events.certainty_note: Emperor Wu's Buddhist patronage is well-documented for the broader period (502-549 CE), but no specific events for 517 CE are documented. The assignment reflects the broader context of his reign.
cross_references: Part of broader Buddhist flourishing in China (5th-6th centuries)

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events for 517 CE are well-documented in surviving sources. The events listed above are based on the broader context of ongoing processes (Vitalian's rebellion, Theodoric's rule, Buddhist patronage) rather than events specifically dated to 517 CE. This reflects the sparse documentation for this particular year.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 517 CE is sparse. No major events are specifically dated to this year in surviving sources. The year falls within well-documented periods (Vitalian's rebellion, Theodoric's reign, Emperor Wu's patronage), but no precise events for 517 CE are recorded. The historical record for this year is essentially a continuation of broader processes documented for adjacent years. This reflects the general sparsity of documentation for individual years in the early 6th century, where events are often dated to broader periods or years rather than specific dates.

graph_edges.note: Vitalian's rebellion continued until suppressed under Justin I's reign (518-527 CE)
Theodoric's rule in 517 CE preceded his death in 526 CE and the subsequent Ostrogothic-Byzantine war
